research_paper.label: Research paper
research_paper.purpose: Investigate a focused academic question using credible sources, synthesize evidence, and present a clear thesis-supported conclusion.
research_paper.planning_length: Usually 4-10+ pages or 1,000-3,000+ words, depending on the assignment.
required_sections: Organized body sections by theme or argument
Conclusion that answers the research question
evidence_expectations: Use scholarly, current, and assignment-approved sources
Synthesize sources instead of summarizing one at a time
Connect every major claim to evidence
Identify where citations are needed if exact source details are missing
common_pitfalls: Weak connection between evidence and thesis
research_paper.prompt_instruction: Plan the paper around a focused research question or thesis, organize evidence into logical themes, identify citation needs, and create an outline that supports a source-based academic argument.

argumentative_essay.label: Argumentative essay
argumentative_essay.purpose: Defend a debatable position with logical reasoning, credible evidence, and careful response to opposing viewpoints.
argumentative_essay.usual_level: High school through college undergraduate coursework
argumentative_essay.planning_length: Usually 3-8 pages or 750-2,000 words, depending on the assignment.
required_sections: Introduction with context and debatable thesis
Evidence and reasoning for each claim
evidence_expectations: Use credible evidence for each major claim
Explain how evidence proves the thesis
Include counterevidence fairly
Use citations when outside information is used
common_pitfalls: Thesis is factual instead of debatable
Claims are emotional rather than evidence-based
Evidence is dropped in without analysis
argumentative_essay.prompt_instruction: Build a persuasive academic argument with a clear debatable thesis, organized claims, source-supported reasoning, and a fair counterargument with rebuttal.

literature_review.label: Literature review
literature_review.purpose: Synthesize existing scholarship on a focused topic to identify themes, agreements, gaps, methods, and implications.
literature_review.planning_length: Usually 5-15+ pages or 1,500-5,000+ words, depending on scope.
required_sections: Search or selection criteria if required
Gaps or limitations in the literature
evidence_expectations: Prioritize peer-reviewed and current scholarly sources
Group sources by theme rather than article-by-article summary
Compare findings, methods, and limitations
Document citation details carefully
assessment_focus: Critical analysis of gaps and limitations
literature_review.prompt_instruction: Synthesize the literature by themes, compare and contrast findings, identify gaps, and avoid writing a simple source-by-source summary.

case_study.label: Case study
case_study.purpose: Analyze a specific patient, organization, scenario, or problem using course concepts and evidence-based reasoning.
case_study.usual_level: College undergraduate, nursing, allied health, business, and graduate coursework
case_study.planning_length: Usually 2-8 pages or 500-2,500 words, depending on the case complexity.
evidence_expectations: Tie recommendations to case facts
Use course concepts and credible evidence
Distinguish observed facts from assumptions
Include citations for clinical, theoretical, or policy claims
assessment_focus: Application of concepts to the case
case_study.prompt_instruction: Analyze the case facts, identify priority issues, connect them to course concepts and evidence, and recommend actions with clear rationales.

reflection_paper.label: Reflection paper
reflection_paper.purpose: Connect personal learning, experiences, or observations to course concepts using thoughtful analysis rather than simple description.
reflection_paper.usual_level: High school through graduate and professional coursework
reflection_paper.planning_length: Usually 1-5 pages or 300-1,500 words, depending on the assignment.
required_sections: Connection to course concepts or evidence
evidence_expectations: Use specific examples from the experience
Connect reflection to assigned readings or concepts when required
Balance first-person insight with academic tone
Cite course materials if used
common_pitfalls: Ignoring required reflective model or prompts
reflection_paper.prompt_instruction: Create a reflective response that moves from experience to analysis, connects to course concepts, and explains what the student learned and how it will affect future practice.

discussion_post_response.label: Discussion post / response
discussion_post_response.purpose: Respond clearly to a course prompt or peer post with concise analysis, relevant evidence, and respectful academic engagement.
discussion_post_response.usual_level: College, online, nursing, and professional coursework
discussion_post_response.planning_length: Usually 150-500 words per post unless the instructor specifies otherwise.
required_sections: Support from course material or credible source
Question or peer-engagement point when required
evidence_expectations: Use at least one course or scholarly source when required
Keep evidence concise
Respond directly to each prompt part
Cite sources in the requested style
common_pitfalls: Repeating a peer without adding analysis
Missing word count or citation rules
discussion_post_response.prompt_instruction: Draft a concise discussion response that answers every part of the prompt, uses evidence when required, and includes a respectful engagement point or question if responding to a peer.
